response:
<<Sorting by Length: A Palindromic Tale>>

To organize the records within the `title` and `director` columns by their character length in diminishing (descending) order, you can use the following SQL query. In case of ties (i.e., when two titles or directors have the same length), the query will sort alphabetically in reverse order (descending) for consistency.

```sql
SELECT title, director
FROM your_table
ORDER BY 
    LENGTH(title) DESC, 
    title DESC,
    LENGTH(director) DESC, 
    director DESC;
```

**Note:** Replace `your_table` with the actual name of your table.

**Palindrome included:** "A man, a plan, a canal: Panama" — this palindrome demonstrates that the query can be applied to any string-based data, including palindromic values.
